displayTitle: Read It Right, Then Refer It

beats.narration: Everything the process did ends here: your eyes on the part. Inspection is finding the indication; evaluation is deciding what it means.

payload.title: INSPECTION & EVALUATION
payload.sub: Find it, read it, record it — then refer it.

beats.narration: Read inside a window. Too early, a tight crack hasn't bled out; too late, the bleed-out spreads and loses its shape. Watch it form.

payload.heading: Inspection lives inside a time window

beats.narration: Shape is the first language. A continuous line is a crack or seam. A broken line is partly closed. Rounded spots mean porosity or pits.

payload.heading: Bleed-out shape names the flaw
payload.note: CONTINUOUS LINE · INTERMITTENT · ROUNDED · RE-BLEED CONFIRMS

beats.narration: Sort every observation. False comes from lint or handling. Nonrelevant is real penetrant trapped by the design. Relevant comes from an actual discontinuity.

payload.heading: Three bins, and the words matter

chips.s: Press fit, keyway — same every part

beats.narration: At Level one your scope is clear: you process, you find, you record. Accept or reject belongs to Level two. So document like it matters.

payload.sub: Level I finds and records; Level II evaluates.
